meta.title: Draft note jumps to the wrong row after adding at the top
meta.summary: The list updates, but edit state and draft text jump to the wrong row because `v-for` is keyed by index instead of item identity.
signals: Text typed into one row appears under a different row after insert
Edit mode sticks to the wrong item after reordering

context.symptom: A manager starts editing a note for Maya, then adds a new teammate to the top of the list. The typed draft is now shown under Noah instead, even though Maya was the one being edited.
context.userImpact: Users stop trusting inline editing because the UI makes it look like the wrong person is being changed.
context.environment: Vue 3 team-management screen with editable rows. The list can prepend, filter, and sort members, and each row has local edit state and a draft note input.

evidence.title: What the user sees
evidence.body: As soon as a new row is inserted at the top, the draft and edit mode appear under a different teammate, even though no save happened.

evidence.title: Render clue
evidence.body: The row component instance is being reused for the new position instead of staying attached to the same logical teammate.

evidence.title: Current list rendering
evidence.body:
<MemberRow
  v-for="(member, index) in members"
  :key="index"
  :member="member"
/>

<button @click="members.unshift(createMember())">
  Add to top
</button>

stages.title: Stage 1: likely root cause
stages.prompt: Why does the draft jump to the wrong row?
stages.helperText: Pick the explanation that matches Vue list identity, not just generic form bugs.

options.label: Vue probably caches inputs too aggressively when lists grow, so old text can appear in the wrong row for a moment.
options.feedback: The issue is not generic input caching. The stronger clue is that the wrong row identity is being reused.

options.label: The insert animation is probably delaying the real rerender, so the old row content stays visible too long.
options.feedback: Animation can affect timing, but it does not explain why edit state moves to a different logical item.

options.label: The list is keyed by index, so after insert Vue reuses the wrong row DOM and local row state for a different teammate.
options.feedback: Correct. In Vue, keys represent identity. `index` tracks position, so inserts and reorders make row state jump to the wrong item.

options.label: `v-model` is not reliable inside `v-for`, so inline editing should move to a modal instead.
options.feedback: `v-model` is fine here. The problem is the unstable key, not the fact that the input lives in a list.

stages.title: Stage 2: debug priority order
stages.prompt: What would you check first? Put the best three at the top.
stages.helperText: First prove that the wrong logical row is being reused after the insert.

candidates.label: Reproduce with one row in edit mode, then insert at the top and observe which logical item keeps the draft.
Inspect the `v-for` key and confirm whether it is based on `index` or a stable item id.
Try a quick repro with `:key="member.id"` and see whether the draft stays with the right row.
candidates.description: A minimal change can confirm the root cause fast.

candidates.label: Add `nextTick()` around the insert path before confirming the identity bug.
Force the whole list to remount on every update before measuring what Vue is reusing.
candidates.description: Masks the bug instead of explaining it.

stages.title: Stage 3: best fix set
stages.prompt: Which changes belong in the real fix?
stages.helperText: Pick the changes that keep row state attached to the correct logical item.

options.label: Key each row by a stable unique id such as `member.id` instead of `index`.
options.feedback: Correct. This gives Vue the real identity signal it needs.

options.label: Clear every draft whenever the list changes so users never notice rows jumping.
options.feedback: That hides the symptom by destroying user work, but it does not fix the wrong identity mapping.

options.label: If edit state must outlive reorders, tie it to `member.id` instead of current array position.
options.feedback: Correct. Any longer-lived row state should follow the item id, not the row index.

options.label: Keep inserts, filters, and sorts working against stable item ids so the row identity stays clear across updates.
options.feedback: Correct. Stable identity is the key design rule here.

options.label: Wrap the list in a changing parent `:key` so Vue remounts every row on every update.
options.feedback: That wipes local state and performance to hide the bug. The right fix is stable keys.

stages.title: Stage 4: regression guard
stages.prompt: What is the best guardrail for this bug?
stages.helperText: Pick the check that would catch row identity drifting after list inserts or reorders.

options.label: Add a component test that edits row B, inserts a new row at the top, and asserts the draft still belongs to row B.
options.feedback: Correct. This directly guards the identity bug users felt.

options.label: Take a screenshot after a refresh and compare it with the expected list order.
options.feedback: That can miss the bug because refresh is one of the things that resets the wrong local row state.

options.label: Track insert API timing because slower inserts make row drift more likely.
options.feedback: The core bug is local list identity, not network timing.

options.label: Avoid inline editing in all Vue lists so key bugs can never happen again.
options.feedback: Inline editing is not the problem. The wrong key is.

scoreBands.label: You saw the wrong row update, but not the identity bug
scoreBands.summary: The UI looked flaky, but the real issue was still how the list keyed item identity.

scoreBands.label: Good instincts, but one fix still treated the symptom
scoreBands.summary: You connected the bug to list rendering, but one step still leaned on resets or remounts.

scoreBands.label: You diagnosed the row-identity bug clearly
scoreBands.summary: You found the unstable key, chose a stable identity fix, and protected the exact user-facing failure.

idealRunbook: Reproduce the bug with one row in edit mode and one insert at the top.
Check whether the list is keyed by position or by stable item id.
Switch to stable keys and keep longer-lived row state tied to item ids.
Lock the fix with a test that inserts or reorders while one row is being edited.

teachingBlocks.title: Vue shortcut
teachingBlocks.text: When row state jumps after insert, reorder, or filter, ask immediately whether the key represents identity or only position.

teachingBlocks.title: Strong answer signals
items: You explain that keys represent identity, not order.
You mention local row state and DOM reuse, not just 'Vue rerendered weirdly'.
You fix the key instead of forcing remounts.

debrief.optionalReflectionPrompt: When is using `index` as a key actually safe, and why is that rare in real apps?
